body.para: Help & Manual (EC Software) is the documentation authoring tool that produces this help project. Every page you are reading is a Help & Manual topic; the project organizes those topics into the table of contents and compiles them to the published output.

The project is one topic per subject. Each topic is an XML file with a title, a keyword index, and a body; the table of contents is a separate map that nests the topics into the eight-section structure. Pages cross-reference each other through topic links rather than copied text, so a fact lives on one page and is linked from the others.

A consistent house style keeps the project uniform: body text uses the Normal style, section headings use the Heading styles, console and code text uses the Code Example style, and a See Also block of topic links closes most pages. Two rules matter for searchability and portability -- the keyword index on each page names every noun on that page, and the text is ASCII-only (a double hyphen stands in for a dash). These are the same conventions the rest of this documentation follows.
